title: Main Kernel Lifecycle
description: Electron desktop host concern — **Main Kernel Lifecycle**. Local mode bootstraps an SDK host context (`createSdkContext`) in the main process and exposes IPC-backed ports; remote mode reuses the HTTP client stack. The main process owns one SDK host context per locally opened project.
context: Electron desktop host concern — **Main Kernel Lifecycle**. Local mode bootstraps an SDK host context (`createSdkContext`) in the main process and exposes IPC-backed ports; remote mode reuses the HTTP client stack. The main process owns one SDK host context per locally opened project.

rules.requirement: one kernel per open local project
rules: Opening a project directory MUST construct exactly one SDK host context (kernel + `createGraphProvider` from `createSdkContext`) for that `specd.yaml` root until the project is closed or switched.

rules.requirement: project switch tears down kernel and graph state
rules: Switching projects MUST dispose the previous SDK host context and any desktop graph provider state before creating a new one to avoid cross-project leakage.

rules.requirement: desktop startup prepares the Electron SQLite graph runtime
rules:
The desktop host MUST prepare its Electron-native SQLite graph runtime before the
app starts local graph work.

The desktop package MUST:

- depend on `@specd/code-graph-electron` for desktop-local graph execution
- expose `rebuild:graph-sqlite-electron` for rebuilding the locally generated
  vendored Electron SQLite addon
- expose `rebuild:graph-electron` as an alias of that rebuild
- run the Electron SQLite rebuild from `prestart`

Desktop startup MUST NOT assume that the vendored sqlite tree or its Electron-targeted
native addon are present in git. The first successful desktop start on a fresh clone
MAY require a local native rebuild when the generated addon is missing or stale for
the current Electron version, platform, or architecture.

This wiring isolates the native SQLite runtime required by the Electron desktop
host without retargeting CLI or API away from `@specd/code-graph`.

rules.requirement: desktop main process launches as Electron
rules:
The desktop `start` script MUST clear `ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE` before spawning Electron so the main process receives the Electron API (`app`, `BrowserWindow`, `ipcMain`) instead of the npm CLI path export.

When `ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE` is set, startup MUST NOT proceed with a broken main process that cannot call `app.whenReady()`.

rules.requirement: main process entry is bundled for pnpm and Electron
rules:
The Electron main entry MUST be built as a bundled CommonJS artifact at `dist/main/index.cjs`.

The bundle MUST externalise `electron`, `@specd/code-graph-electron`, `@specd/sdk`, and
`@specd/client`, relying on workspace `exports.require` surfaces for correct pnpm
resolution under the Electron main process.

`package.json` `main` MUST reference `dist/main/index.cjs`.

`createSdkContext` for the desktop main process MUST receive `logFormatter: createLogFormatter({ colorize: false })` so that log readback through local IPC returns plain text without ANSI escape sequences.

scenarios.requirement: one kernel per open local project
scenarios.name: Local open uses createSdkContext
when: the user opens a local project directory
then: the main process awaits createSdkContext from @specd/sdk once for that project root
AND it does not call createKernel from @specd/core directly

scenarios.requirement: project switch tears down kernel and graph state
scenarios.name: Opening new folder disposes old kernel
then: kernel A disposed
AND kernel B serves IPC

scenarios.requirement: project switch tears down kernel and graph state
scenarios.name: Graph provider reset on switch
then: in-memory graph cache cleared
AND status reflects new project paths

scenarios.requirement: project switch tears down kernel and graph state
scenarios.name: In-flight IPC cancelled on switch
given: long IPC call running for project A
then: A requests rejected or aborted
AND B calls do not read A paths

scenarios.requirement: desktop startup prepares the Electron SQLite graph runtime
scenarios.name: Desktop package exposes Electron graph rebuild wiring
when: the desktop package scripts are inspected
then: rebuild:graph-sqlite-electron rebuilds the locally generated vendored
AND rebuild:graph-electron aliases that rebuild
AND prestart executes the Electron SQLite rebuild before app startup

scenarios.requirement: desktop startup prepares the Electron SQLite graph runtime
scenarios.name: Desktop startup does not require git-tracked vendor artifacts
given: a fresh clone without committed vendored sqlite artifacts
then: it triggers the Electron sqlite rebuild workflow

AND desktop local graph startup does not depend on vendored sqlite files being
present in git

scenarios.requirement: desktop startup prepares the Electron SQLite graph runtime
scenarios.name: Desktop local host depends on the Electron graph package
when: the desktop package dependencies are inspected
then: @specd/code-graph-electron is present for desktop-local graph execution

scenarios.requirement: desktop startup prepares the Electron SQLite graph runtime
scenarios.name: CLI and API keep the standard graph package
when: CLI and API package dependencies are inspected
then: they depend on @specd/code-graph
AND they do not depend on @specd/code-graph-electron

scenarios.requirement: desktop main process launches as Electron
scenarios.name: start clears ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE
then: the spawned Electron process can access app.whenReady
AND require('electron') in the main bundle is not the npm CLI path string

scenarios.requirement: main process entry is bundled for pnpm and Electron
scenarios.name: main entry uses bundled CJS artifact
then: main is dist/main/index.cjs
AND tsup externalises electron, @specd/code-graph-electron, @specd/sdk, and @specd/client

scenarios.name: Local desktop logs are formatted without colors
then: logFormatter has colorize set to false
AND log messages do not contain ANSI escape control codes
